mnemonics.title: Preorder-Inorder-Postorder Memory
mnemonics.content: Remember as 'Root First' (Pre), 'Root Between' (In), 'Root Last' (Post). Mnemonic: 'You любите In между (between) left and right, Pre before, Post после (after).'

mnemonics.title: Threaded Tree Types
mnemonics.content: Single threads point one direction only; double threads point both. One-way: left null→predecessor OR right null→successor. Two-way: both nulls become threads for O(1) successor finding.

mnemonics.title: Level Order Queue Rule
mnemonics.content: First-In-First-Out ensures parents processed before children. Enqueue children left-to-right maintains level order. Dequeue current, enqueue its children. Queue empties when all levels processed.

acronyms.usage: Level-order traversal is BFS applied to trees; explores all nodes at distance d before distance d+1
Covers preorder, inorder, postorder traversals; explores depth before siblings using stack (explicit or call stack)
Threading technique achieving O(1) space by temporarily linking nodes to their inorder predecessors

visualTips.visualization: Imagine stack frames growing downward: root at top, deeper nodes below. At leaf, stack unwinds (pops) returning to parent. Height = maximum stack size = tree depth.

Picture horizontal layers: top row = level 0 (root), second row = level 1 children. Queue shows nodes currently waiting: imagine a pipeline where nodes enter left, exit right after processing children.
Draw dashed arrows from null child positions to inorder predecessor/successor. These 'threads' create a linear path through tree enabling O(1) traversal without recursion.

analogies.analogy: Recursion stack is like climbing a tree branch by branch before returning (depth-first). Queue is like checking each floor of a building completely before going to next floor (breadth-first).
Expression trees are like assembly lines: postorder = workers complete all subtasks before passing to next (bottom-up), preorder = manager gives top-level task first (top-down).

analogies.visualization: Morris traversal is like temporarily adding shortcuts on a hiking trail so you can return without remembering the way back - you create the path as you go, then remove traces afterward.
